root_cause.text: The XSLT component inherently trusted the user-supplied `CamelXsltResourceUri` message header to dynamically locate and load an XSLT resource, which could allow an attacker to inject and execute an arbitrary, potentially malicious XSLT stylesheet.
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes the logic in `XsltEndpoint.java` where `String newResourceUri = exchange.getIn().getHeader(XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI, String.class);` was read and used to invoke `findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri);`, and deprecates `XSLT_RESOURCE_URI` in `XsltConstants.java`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The XSLT component inherently trusted the user-supplied `CamelXsltResourceUri` message header to dynamically locate and load an XSLT resource, which could allow an attacker to inject and execute an arbitrary, potentially malicious XSLT stylesheet.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes the logic in `XsltEndpoint.java` where `String newResourceUri = exchange.getIn().getHeader(XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI, String.class);` was read and used to invoke `findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri);`, and deprecates `XSLT_RESOURCE_URI` in `XsltConstants.java`.

intent_record.what: Adds a @Deprecated annotation and an explanatory comment to the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI constant.
intent_record.how: Signals to developers and tooling that the constant should no longer be used, without changing actual runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove dynamic endpoint creation based on XSLT resource URI headers.
intent_record.what: Removes the logic that extracts a dynamic XSLT resource URI from the exchange header and creates a new endpoint.
intent_record.how: Prevents the endpoint from dynamically redirecting exchanges to newly created endpoints based on header values.

intent_record.intent_summary: Ignore test for unsupported XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header feature.
intent_record.what: Added the @Ignore annotation to the XsltFromHeaderOverrideEndpointUriTest class with a message explaining the feature is unsupported.
intent_record.how: Prevents the test from executing during the build process, avoiding test failures caused by the removal of XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header support.

grouping_result.intent: Ignore test for unsupported XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header feature.
Remove dynamic endpoint creation based on XSLT resource URI headers.

consistency_review.core_intent: Handle the unsupported XSLT_RESOURCE_URI feature by deprecating its constant and ignoring its associated test.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the vulnerability by deprecating the constant and ignoring tests for the XSLT_RESOURCE_URI header, which is the root cause of the arbitrary XSLT resource loading issue.

The group's changes directly remove the vulnerable behavior by eliminating dynamic endpoint creation based on the user-supplied XSLT resource URI headers, which is the exact root cause of the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This statement extracts the user-supplied 'CamelXsltResourceUri' from the message header, which is the root cause of the dynamic XSLT resource loading.
This statement dynamically locates and creates a new endpoint using the user-supplied resource URI, leading to arbitrary XSLT resource loading.
This statement executes the exchange on the dynamically created endpoint, effectively applying the injected malicious XSLT stylesheet.

vuln_statements.explanation: Extracts the untrusted user-supplied 'CamelXsltResourceUri' header which allows arbitrary XSLT resources to be specified.
Dynamically finds or creates a new endpoint using the untrusted resource URI, enabling the loading of malicious stylesheets.
Executes the exchange on the dynamically created endpoint, applying the potentially malicious XSLT stylesheet.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/camel/
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 4abc7be95f5a39c4ab0781da53e89b13fe863990
Author: Jonathan Anstey <janstey@apache.org>
Date:   Wed Nov 16 18:09:22 2011 +0000

    CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/camel/trunk@1202819 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/camel-core/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/xslt/XsltEndpoint.java b/camel-core/src/main/java/org/apache/camel/component/xslt/XsltEndpoint.java
index a9b9a4a8ac4.
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces the logic to read the `XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI` header from the exchange (`exchange.getIn().getHeader(XsltConstants.XSLT_RESOURCE_URI, String.class)`) and dynamically creates a new endpoint with this URI without any validation or restriction (`findOrCreateEndpoint(getEndpointUri(), newResourceUri)`). This directly causes the vulnerability described in the root cause.

    CAMEL-4689 - Add option to override XSLT stylesheet used via header
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/camel/trunk@1202819 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

This allows an attacker to load an arbitrary XSLT stylesheet.
